dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9118 IH: Enhanced Alerts for Missing Loved Ones with Disabilities Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Alford (for himself and Mr. Smith of Missouri) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend Kristen’s Act to provide for the inclusion of information related to developmental disabilities, alzheimer’s disease, and dementia, in alerts issued through the Ashanti Alert communications network, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Enhanced Alerts for Missing Loved Ones with Disabilities Act of 2026.

section.header: Alerts for missing adults with developmental disabilities, alzheimer’s disease, and dementia
section.text: Section 204(b) of Kristen's Act (34 U.S.C. 21904(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), by inserting after (including health care needs the following: and whether the individual has a developmental disability (as such term is defined in section 102 of the Developmental Disabilities Assistance and Bill of Rights Act of 2000 (42 U.S.C. 15002), alzheimer’s disease, or dementia; and

in paragraph (3), by striking suffers from a proven mental or physical disability and inserting the following: has been diagnosed with a mental disability, a physical disability, a developmental disability, alzheimer’s disease, or dementia.

section.header: Training related to developmentally disabled missing persons

subsection.text: Section 206 of Kristen's Act (34 U.S.C. 21906) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (2), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:
training and educational programs related to responding effectively to a reported missing adult with a developmental disability.

subsection.text: Section 301(b) of the PROTECT Act (34 U.S.C. 20501(b)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (3), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (4), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

provide training and educational programs for use by States, territories of the United States, and tribal governments, related to responding effectively to a reported missing child with a developmental disability.
